suite.name: 05 Alerts End To End

kw.name: Get Time
kw.doc: Returns the given time in the requested format.

kw.msg: POST Request : url=http://localhost:51734/v1/auth/signup path_url=/v1/auth/signup headers={'User-Agent': 'python-requests/2.33.1', 'Accept-Encoding': 'gzip, deflate', 'Accept': '*/*', 'Connection': 'keep-alive', 'Content-Length': '116', 'Content-Type': 'application/json'} body=b'{"email": "e2e-alerts-1776427657@tennetctl.test", "password": "pass-mon-alerts-E2E!1", "display_name": "Alerts E2E"}'
kw.doc: Sends a POST request on a previously created HTTP Session.

kw.name: New Browser
kw.msg: {"browser": "chromium", "headless": true, "chromiumSandbox": false, "devtools": false, "handleSIGHUP": true, "handleSIGINT": true, "handleSIGTERM": true, "slowMo": 0.0, "timeout": 30000.0, "tracesDir": "/Users/sri/Documents/tennetctl/artifacts/13_monitoring_alerts_e2e/browser/traces/temp/e0deab6d-c31d-4de6-a40c-68ee9dc614d0"}

test.name: Alert Rule Fires When Threshold Breached

kw.name: POST On Session
kw.msg: POST Request : url=http://localhost:51734/v1/monitoring/alert-rules path_url=/v1/monitoring/alert-rules headers={'User-Agent': 'python-requests/2.33.1', 'Accept-Encoding': 'gzip, deflate', 'Accept': '*/*', 'Connection': 'keep-alive', 'Content-Type': 'application/json', 'x-org-id': '019d9660-8b03-7370-b4ac-c54b2b1d9986', 'x-user-id': '019d9b56-eac2-7be3-99f3-de83e4f965fa', 'Content-Length': '402'} body=b'{"name": "e2e-fire-1776427657", "target": "metrics", "dsl": {"target": "metrics", "metric_key": "e2ealerts1776427657", "aggregate": "sum", "bucket": "1m", "timerange": {"last": "15m"}}, "condition": {"op": "gt", "threshold": "0", "for_duration_seconds": "0"}, "severity": "critical", "notify_template_key": "test.alert.template", "labels": {"recipient_user_id": "019d9b56-eac2-7be3-99f3-de83e4f965fa"}}'
kw.doc: Sends a POST request on a previously created HTTP Session.

kw.name: Set Variable
kw.doc: Returns the given values which can then be assigned to a variables.

POST an alert rule directly via the backend API. Uses the test.alert.template notify_template_key placeholder — the evaluator handles notify failures gracefully (alert event is still created and visible in the UI).

kw.doc: Makes a variable available everywhere within the scope of the current suite.

test.doc: Seed a rule with threshold 0 on an existing counter → within one evaluator cycle, an alert event is created and visible on /monitoring/alerts.

kw.doc: Returns and logs the length of the given item as an integer.

kw.name: Should Be True
kw.arg: Expected >=1 silence after creating via UI, got ${count}
kw.doc: Fails if the given condition is not true.

test.doc: Click the Silence button in an alert row → fill reason → save → row shows the silenced badge after refetch.

test.doc: On /monitoring/alerts/rules/new, fill the form, save — the new rule appears on the rules list.

test.doc: Open an existing rule via the list → editor loads with current values → update severity → save succeeds.

kw.name: Close Browser
kw.doc: Closes the current browser.

suite.doc: Monitoring Alerts — seed a metric + a rule → evaluator fires → alert surfaces in UI → silence suppresses notifications → editor creates new rule. Requires backend started with TENNETCTL_MONITORING_ALERT_EVAL_INTERVAL_S=10 so cycles complete within the E2E Sleep windows.
